evals.prompt: Add ID Capture to my React Native app so I can scan passports, driver's licenses and national ID cards and read the holder's name and date of birth. Here's my screen file: EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes a complete ID Capture integration: DataCaptureContext.initialize() + sharedInstance, IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments and a FullDocumentScanner wrapped in IdCaptureScanner, new IdCapture(settings) + object-literal listener (didCaptureId/didRejectId), <DataCaptureView> + new IdCaptureOverlay attached via ref, IdCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings(), and an AppState-based camera lifecycle.

assertions.text: '-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
settings.acceptedDocuments is configured with document objects like Passport, DriverLicense, IdCard (semantic)
the listener is a plain object literal with didCaptureId and didRejectId methods, not a class implementing an interface (semantic)
addListener is called on the IdCapture instance, awaited (semantic)
the IdCapture mode is attached to the context via context.setMode or context.addMode, with the call awaited (semantic)
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription for iOS (semantic)
Setup checklist mentions PermissionsAndroid or the Android CAMERA runtime permission (semantic)
settings.scannerType is NOT used in the final code (semantic)
IdCaptureRegion.Any or another PascalCase IdCaptureRegion member is used, not the lowercase Flutter-style form (semantic)

evals.prompt: I only need to read the PDF417 barcode on the back of US driver's licenses — nothing else. Set up ID Capture for that in EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts only DriverLicense for IdCaptureRegion.Us and uses a SingleSideScanner with only the barcode flag enabled, wrapped in IdCaptureScanner.

assertions.text: the SingleSideScanner enables only the barcode flag, i.e. SingleSideScanner(true, false, false) (semantic)
data from the barcode is read, either via capturedId.barcode or via the harmonized top-level fields (documentNumber, fullName, dateOfBirth, etc.) that the barcode populates (semantic)

evals.prompt: Set up ID Capture to scan passports and extract the MRZ data (full name, document number, date of expiry). Use EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts Passport, uses a scanner that reads the MRZ, and reads fields from the captured id / mrz result. The MRZ result is accessed via capturedId.mrzResult (not capturedId.mrz, which is the Flutter name).

assertions.text: the scanner reads the MRZ, via FullDocumentScanner or a SingleSideScanner with machineReadableZone enabled (semantic)
capturedId.mrz is NOT used (the Flutter name) (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add ID Capture but anonymize sensitive data — I don't want document images or sensitive fields in the result. Use EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets IdCaptureSettings.anonymizationMode to an IdAnonymizationMode value that covers fields and images.

assertions.text: an anonymization mode covering fields and images is chosen, e.g. IdAnonymizationMode.FieldsAndImages (semantic)

evals.prompt: I need the cropped document image and the face image back from each scanned ID, and I want to render them in a React Native <Image>. Set that up in EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables image extraction via setShouldPassImageTypeToResult for IdImageType.Face and IdImageType.CroppedDocument, reads capturedId.images, and renders the base64 strings via the data: URI form (since IdImages returns base64 strings on RN, not URIs or Image components).

assertions.text: the cropped document image is read via images.getCroppedDocument(IdSide.Front) — not a non-existent .croppedDocument getter (semantic)
the base64 image is rendered using the data:image/png;base64, URI form on a React Native Image, not via .buffer / .bytes (semantic)

evals.prompt: We want to reject voided / cancelled US driver's licenses during ID Capture. How do I enable that? My screen is EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-react-native-datacapture-id-voided-detection package, sets settings.rejectVoidedIds = true, and handles RejectionReason.DocumentVoided in didRejectId.

assertions.text: the scandit-react-native-datacapture-id-voided-detection package is added as a dependency (semantic)

evals.prompt: We scan European driving licenses and need the vehicle categories from the back of the card. Set up ID Capture for that in EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-react-native-datacapture-id-europe-driving-license package, sets settings.decodeBackOfEuropeanDrivingLicense = true with a scanner that reads the back, and reads capturedId.vizResult.drivingLicenseDetails.drivingLicenseCategories, using category.code (the documented getter), not the non-existent categoryCode.

assertions.text: each category's data is read via the .code getter, not a non-existent .categoryCode (semantic)
a scanner that reads the back of the card is used, e.g. FullDocumentScanner (semantic)
the scandit-react-native-datacapture-id-europe-driving-license package is added as a dependency (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add forged-barcode detection for US driver's licenses (AAMVA) to my ID Capture setup so we can flag likely-forged IDs. Use EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-react-native-datacapture-id-aamva-barcode-verification package, sets settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true, and/or reads capturedId.verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification with AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus. It does NOT use a standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier class (removed on RN at v8).

assertions.text: verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification is referenced, or RejectionReason.ForgedAamvaBarcode is handled (semantic)
AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus is referenced when inspecting the result (semantic)
the scandit-react-native-datacapture-id-aamva-barcode-verification package is added as a dependency (semantic)
a standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier class is NOT used in the code (semantic)

evals.prompt: How should I handle the case where a document is detected but can't be captured? I want to show the user a clear message. My screen is EmptyScreen.tsx and I accept passports and driver's licenses.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements didRejectId and maps RejectionReason values (Timeout, NotAcceptedDocumentType, etc.) to user-facing messages, disabling capture while the message is shown.

assertions.text: rejection reasons are mapped to user-facing messages, e.g. via a switch on RejectionReason (semantic)
idCapture.isEnabled is set to false while the rejection message is shown (semantic)

evals.prompt: We want ID Capture to reject documents whose printed data doesn't match the barcode or MRZ — data-consistency verification. Enable it and show me how to read the consistency result. My screen is EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.rejectInconsistentData = true, handles RejectionReason.InconsistentData in didRejectId, and reads capturedId.verificationResult.dataConsistency (allChecksPassed / failedChecks / frontReviewImage). No standalone DataConsistencyVerifier class is used (it does not exist on RN).

assertions.text: a standalone DataConsistencyVerifier class is NOT used in the code (semantic)

evals.prompt: I need to scan mobile driver's licenses (mDL, ISO 18013-5) in my RN app and read the holder's name and date of birth. Set that up in EmptyScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner(undefined, new MobileDocumentScanner(true, false)) for mobile documents, and reads the mobile-document data via capturedId.mobileDocument (or the harmonized top-level fields). MobileDocumentScanner ships in the base package, with no add-on dependency.

assertions.text: the MobileDocumentScanner is passed as the second positional argument to IdCaptureScanner, i.e. IdCaptureScanner(undefined, new MobileDocumentScanner(...)) (semantic)
capturedId.mobileDocument is read, or the harmonized top-level fields the mobile document populates are read (semantic)
